trial_details.trajectory:
The pod is in the 'ErrImageNeverPull' state, which indicates an issue with the image being pulled.Based on the pod description, the issue is that the image 'misspeling-app:latset' cannot be pulled, as the pod has a pull policy of 'Never'. This is likely due to a misspelling in the image name and tag.I fixed the issue by patching the deployment to use the correct image name 'misspelling-app:latest' instead of the misspelled 'misspeling-app:latset'.

FIX COMPLETE

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-lm87k -n case6-test
   Output: NAME                                      READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE
misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-lm87k   0/1     ErrImageNeverPull   0          12s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-lm87k -n case6-test
   Output: Name:             misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-lm87k
Namespace:        case6-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Thu, 25 D...
3. $ kubectl patch deployment misspelling-deployment -n case6-test --type=json -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image", "value": "misspelling-app:latest"}]'
   Output: deployment.apps/misspelling-deployment patched...
